description:
Synthesize a program-wide `fz_spawn_thunk(c)` fn in `ir_lower` and
rewrite every `spawn(x)` callsite to spawn a wrapper closure that
captures `x` and tail-calls it. This decouples spawn's deferred-
execution contract from the closure target's ABI, making the .29.8
native-eligibility lift safe in any module (including concurrent ones).

## The wrapper

```
fn fz_spawn_thunk(c) do
  c()    // lowers to TailCallClosure(c, [])
end
```

Single program-wide fn (or lazily synthesized on first spawn — zero
overhead in spawn-free programs). Has `TailCallClosure` so it's
parking-reachable by today's rules → stays uniform-ABI without any
analysis change.

## The rewrite

At each `Prim::Builtin(Spawn, [v])` lowering site:

  Builtin(Spawn, [v])
    →
  let w = MakeClosure(fz_spawn_thunk_id, [v]);
  Builtin(Spawn, [w])

`v` is the user-passed closure (after the existing bare-fn-name →
`MakeClosure(target, [])` adapter at ir_lower.rs:543). The wrapper has
1 capture (the user's closure) and 0 call args.

## Why this works

`Runtime::spawn_closure` (src/runtime.rs:238) invokes the closure's
stub with `cont_ptr=null` purely to **materialize an initial frame**
for the child task — the body executes later, in the child's quantum,
when the trampoline dispatches that frame.

With wrapping: the stub invoked is `fz_spawn_thunk`'s. The thunk is
uniform-ABI (parking-reachable), so its stub builds a frame with `c`
in slot 1, returns the frame for trampoline dispatch. Trampoline picks
up the thunk frame in the child's quantum — parent is back in
`rt.tasks` — and dispatches the thunk body. The body's
`TailCallClosure(c, [])` loads stub_fp from the wrapped closure and
call_indirects with `my_cont=null`. The wrapped closure's stub (whether
its target body is uniform or native) takes the cont=null path: uniform
allocates the body frame with cont=null in slot 0 (body's emit_return
halts); native synchronously calls the body, sees cont=null, takes the
halt branch.

End-to-end: spawn's "execute later" semantics are preserved without
any constraint on the user-written closure target's ABI.

## Investigation findings (no surprises)

- `heap::deep_copy_value` HeapKind::Closure (runtime/src/heap.rs:703)
  recursively deep-copies each captured FzValue, so the wrapper's
  capture (the original closure heap ptr) is followed and the original
  closure's captures land in the child heap too.
- `ir_interp.rs:488-505` BuiltinKind::Spawn unpacks the closure as
  `(fn_id, captured)` and runs the spawned task at `fn_id` with
  captures as entry params. With wrapping, `fn_id` is
  `fz_spawn_thunk` and captures is `[original_closure]`. The thunk's
  body then dispatches via TailCallClosure → original closure runs.
  No interp changes required.
- `parking.rs:174-178` already marks any fn with `TailCallClosure` as
  parking-reachable. The thunk qualifies automatically.
- GC: the wrapper closure becomes parent-heap garbage immediately
  after `spawn` returns (the SSA var holding it is dead). Next
  collection sweeps it. No retention.
- The thunk's FnId is allocated lazily during expression lowering
  (single fresh_fn_id; synthesize the FnBuilder body once and add to
  module before mb.build()). Calling `fresh_fn_id` mid second-pass
  is supported (it's just an incrementing counter).
- Typer treatment: the thunk is never `Term::Call`'d from IR (only
  invoked via the closure stub from runtime), so only its any-key
  spec is built — c typed as `any`, return typed as `any`. Existing
  typer machinery covers it.

## Acceptance

- New unit test in `ir_lower`: when a program contains `spawn(child)`,
  the lowered module contains a fn named `fz_spawn_thunk` AND the
  spawn site's arg is a `MakeClosure(fz_spawn_thunk_id, [...])`.
- New unit test: program without any spawn has no `fz_spawn_thunk` fn.
- Existing concurrency fixtures pass unchanged: ping_pong,
  spawn_with_captures, plus runtime.rs spawn-flow tests.
- 373 unit + 5 fixture-matrix tests stay green.
- `cargo build --release` clean (warnings are errors).

## Blocks

fz-ul4.29.8 (native-eligibility lift relies on this wrapping for
spawn-safety).

## Out of scope

- The native-ABI lift itself — that's fz-ul4.29.8.
- Eliminating the wrapper allocation when the closure value is
  statically known not to escape into multiple spawns — future
  optimization; today's cost is one closure alloc + one trampoline
  iter per spawn, dwarfed by spawn's process-creation cost.
title: Wrap spawn-target closures in fz_spawn_thunk
